competitive_position.summary: Zycus is confirmed shortlisted (top 3) as of 22 June 2026. The live peer threats are GEP Worldwide (strongest current threat — best-fit S2P peer in APAC/Middle East, consistently assumed in MEDDPICC and SF fields), SAP Ariba (enterprise S2P incumbent threat, named in SF Competitors__c), and iValua (another enterprise S2P player, named in SF Competitors__c and MEDDPICC). JAGGAER and Coupa are less certain: Coupa was questioned in the MEDDPICC entry ('maybe not Coupa due to scope?'), suggesting it may have faded or been eliminated; JAGGAER appears in SF Competitors__c but has no call or note mention. No buyer commentary on competitor pricing, positioning, or preference has surfaced. The wedge: Zycus's Merlin agentic AI (ANA) for sourcing and negotiation (Tushar expressed strong interest on 11 Dec 2025), and confirmed Epicor integration (11 Feb 2026 demo). The risk: during the 4+ month pause (Feb-June 2026), peers may have delivered exec-level business cases or secured CFO engagement, positioning them ahead post-restart. Visibility into the competitive field is limited — no recent intel, no scoring shared, no indication of buyer preference.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.quote: Not clear yet - assume Ariba, iValua, GEP ...maybe not Coupa due to scope?
competitors.how_we_win: Merlin agentic negotiation agent (ANA) on supplier pricing and autonomous sourcing; make it the Phase 1 centerpiece in deeper demos post-restart, and secure Tushar's confirmation that GEP cannot match agentic AI depth.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.how_we_win: Ariba is complex and heavyweight; position Zycus as the agile, AI-native alternative with faster Epicor integration and phased rollout (no ERP replacement).

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: maybe not Coupa due to scope?
competitors.how_we_win: Coupa likely faded due to scope/fit or pricing concerns (MEDDPICC entry suggests uncertainty). Confirm with Tushar post-restart whether Coupa is still in contention or eliminated.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.how_we_win: iValua is a credible S2P suite; differentiate on agentic AI (not just analytics) and Arabian Industries' specific Epicor integration (confirm iValua cannot match seamless add-on model).

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.how_we_win: JAGGAER appears in SF field but no buyer mention in calls or notes; likely a placeholder or early-stage consideration. Validate status with Tushar post-restart.

champion_strength.source: SF contact role; Avoma calls 11 Dec 2025, 11 Feb 2026; call 22 June 2026; SF Next_Step__c; MEDDPICC entry 11 Feb 2026
champion_strength.summary: Tushar Patel is the sole engaged champion, actively driving the evaluation and maintaining momentum through the exec-imposed pause. He confirmed the shortlist (top 3) on 22 June 2026 and stated the project will restart in July/August 2026. However, he is mid-level (Procurement Specialist, not Head of Procurement), and has not delivered on his 11 Feb 2026 commitment to arrange a CFO and MD pitch (4+ months elapsed). His access to power is unverified, and we have no evidence he is advocating for Zycus vs peers. Champion strength: developing. At risk if CFO/MD deprioritize S2P upon restart or if a peer vendor secures exec engagement first.

sources: MEDDPICC entry 11 Feb 2026: 'CFO is the key decision maker, taking the decision in collaboration with our MD. CFO owns budget.'
SF Next_Step__c 11 Feb 2026: Tushar agreed to arrange CFO and MD pitch after follow-up session
Avoma 11 Feb 2026: 'Arrange a high-level overview call with the CFO (and MD) to review Zycus' (action item)
SF contact roles: Mahesh Nair, Chief Financial Officer
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The CFO (Mahesh Nair) is the identified Economic Buyer, making the decision 'in collaboration with our MD' (MEDDPICC entry 11 Feb 2026). Tushar Patel committed on the 11 Feb 2026 call to arrange a high-level overview call with the CFO and MD after the follow-up Spend Analysis session, stating 'He has agreed that we can meet/pitch Zycus to CFO (+maybe MD) after the next session' (SF Next_Step__c 11 Feb 2026). However, that follow-up session was never scheduled, and as of 22 June 2026 the CFO and MD have not been engaged. The CFO owns the budget but has not validated the $175k investment or confirmed budget availability (MEDDPICC 11 Feb 2026: 'No budget assigned yet; they are still figuring it out'). The EB is mapped but passive; no two-way conversation with the CFO has occurred, no exec-level value narrative delivered, and no sponsorship secured. The gap: the CFO has not bought in, and with the exec team pausing non-essential projects due to the war, there is no evidence the CFO will prioritize S2P upon restart. Next move: upon restart in July/August, the deal owner's manager should engage the CFO directly with a concise value narrative (transparency, risk reduction, procurement efficiency) and validate budget authority and investment appetite before deeper demos resume.

recommended_moves: Upon restart in early July, secure CFO and MD 15-minute value briefing before deeper demos.
Multi-thread to Head of Supply Chain (Shiva Prakash) and Head of Contracting/Sourcing (Khalfan Al Mabsali) by mid-July.
Run competitive review with Tushar in first week of July to surface gaps and positioning vs GEP/Ariba/iValua.
